problem_statement: A county emergency-management intake service stopped generating previews for field teams' HEIC damage photographs after worker hosts were refreshed, although the original photographs continued to upload and remain intact.

observations.statement: Preview failures began only as jobs moved onto hosts built from the 2026.07 base image; two draining hosts on the prior image continued to preview the same photographs successfully.
observations.provenance: Scheduler placement ledger joined to host-image inventory and preview outcomes.

observations.statement: The preserved worker stderr reports that the HEIC decoder cannot load libheif.so.1 on every failed job; no decoder exception appears for successful jobs on prior-image hosts.
observations.provenance: Write-once incident log bundle collected before any host repair.

observations.statement: Signed package inventories show libheif1 present on the prior image and absent from 2026.07 after the image publisher removed the multimedia compatibility group.
observations.provenance: Image publisher manifests verified against the county's artifact registry.

observations.statement: The intake application artifact, launch settings, queue messages, and photograph bytes are identical when a failed job from a new host is replayed successfully on a draining prior-image host.
observations.provenance: Paired replay from a copied queue message with publication disabled.

observations.statement: JPEG and PNG previews remained successful on both image generations, while all 146 HEIC jobs assigned to new-image hosts failed before pixel decoding began.
observations.provenance: Format-stratified preview report derived from intake receipts and worker stage counters.

observations.statement: Adding the exact prior signed libheif1 package to an isolated 2026.07 image restored previews for a 60-file corpus without changing the intake artifact or any launch setting.
observations.provenance: Offline image rebuild and corpus replay with all outbound publication blocked.

observations.statement: Checksums of every original incident photograph match its upload receipt, and malware scanning, object storage, and queue delivery show no errors during the preview outage.
observations.provenance: Independent storage-integrity and security reports retained for the incident window.

observations.statement: A canary host carrying the signed compatibility package completed 500 mixed-format preview jobs under production-like load with no loader error and no output difference from the prior image.
observations.provenance: Safety-reviewed canary run using copied photographs and a private output bucket.

observations.statement: After the rebuilt image was rolled out, the 146-job backlog drained and 2,311 subsequent previews completed over 24 hours with no missing-object message.
observations.provenance: Post-rollout queue reconciliation and worker error summary.
